title: Smart Sensor Fleet Health
spl:
index=iot sourcetype="iot_platform:devices"
| where battery_pct < 20 OR status!="online" OR last_seen < relative_time(now(),"-4h")
| table device_id, device_type, location, battery_pct, status, last_seen
description: IoT sensors with low batteries or offline status create monitoring gaps. Fleet management ensures comprehensive coverage.
value: IoT sensors with low batteries or offline status create monitoring gaps. Fleet management ensures comprehensive coverage.
implementation: Poll IoT platform API for device status. Track battery levels, connectivity, and data freshness. Alert on low battery (<20%) and offline devices (>4 hours). Report on fleet health for maintenance planning.
visualization: Table (devices needing attention), Gauge (fleet health %), Pie chart (device status distribution), Map (device locations with status).
knownFalsePositives: Battery alarms during scheduled load tests, planned battery replacements, or after a sustained brownout recovery.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: IoT platform API input.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IoT platform device management API.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll IoT platform API for device status. Track battery levels, connectivity, and data freshness. Alert on low battery (<20%) and offline devices (>4 hours). Report on fleet health for maintenance planning.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iot sourcetype="iot_platform:devices"
| where battery_pct < 20 OR status!="online" OR last_seen < relative_time(now(),"-4h")
| table device_id, device_type, location, battery_pct, status, last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Smart Sensor Fleet Health** — IoT sensors with low batteries or offline status create monitoring gaps. Fleet management ensures comprehensive coverage.

Documented **Data sources**: IoT platform device management API. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IoT platform API input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iot; **sourcetype**: iot_platform:devices. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iot, sourcetype="iot_platform:devices". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where battery_pct < 20 OR status!="online" OR last_seen < relative_time(now(),"-4h")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Smart Sensor Fleet Health**): table device_id, device_type, location, battery_pct, status, last_seen


Step 3 — Validate
Compare device counts, last-seen times, and health fields to the IoT platform console (for example AWS IoT, Azure IoT Hub, or Google Cloud IoT) for the same period. Verify API polling intervals and that offline rules match your operational definition.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (devices needing attention), Gauge (fleet health %), Pie chart (device status distribution), Map (device locations with status).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your connected devices and the messages they send. We help you know if something is off with batteries, signal, and which sensors have gone quiet before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
